memory.recent_lessons_md:
### 2026-05-13 — PPI shock + PLTR support break

- April PPI +1.4% vs +0.5% expected — largest monthly jump since 2022. S&P rose +0.69% anyway (tech-led, narrow breadth).
- PLTR -4.31% ($136→$129.99). $134 support broken. Re-scored 6.50 (Technical 3). SKIP until reclaim of $134.
- AMD pullback from $477 to $448 normalizes RSI to ~60. Re-score 7.33 (Technical improved from 5→6). MUST ENTER May 14.
- 7th consecutive API-blocked routine. AMD MOC attempted 19:42:21Z — HTTP 403. Guardrail violation continues.
- Trump arrives Beijing. Summit May 14-15. Chip export controls on agenda — potential positive catalyst.
- NVDA +2.1% ($225.40) — China chip deal overrides PPI headwind. Earnings 8 days (May 21).

### 2026-05-12 — CPI +3.7% YoY (hot) + China chip deal

- April CPI at 8:30 AM ET (headline +3.7% expected). No Fed rate cuts until 2027.
- PLTR MOO (36sh, 7.33) and AMD MOO (10sh, 7.33) attempted — HTTP 403 again (6th routine).

### 2026-05-01 — Operator-mandated deployment bias correction

- Score ≥ 7 = enter at next routine. Only 3 valid skip exemptions.

market.macro_notes_md:
- **Trump-Xi Summit Day 1 (May 14):** U.S. approved NVDA H200 chip sales to ~10 Chinese companies (Alibaba, ByteDance, Tencent, JD.com, Lenovo, Foxconn — up to 75,000 chips each). First major hardware approval since 2022 restrictions. Direct catalyst for NVDA (+3%), TSM, AMD (MI308 also cleared), AVGO. Summit Day 2 (May 15) may yield more concessions.
- **Cisco Q3 FY2026 (May 13 AH):** Rev $15.84B (+12% YoY, beat $280M), EPS $1.06 (beat), AI orders raised to $9B target (cumulative $5.3B), product orders +35%, FY2026 guidance raised $1.3B to $62.8-63.0B, ~4,000 job cuts (AI pivot restructuring). CSCO up 15-18% to $101-102. Strongest AI infrastructure confirmation in Q.
- **S&P 500 futures:** +0.3% (~7,474). Nasdaq +0.2%. Dow targeting 50,000. Risk-on.
- **PPI (May 13):** April +1.4% MoM vs +0.5% expected. Annual ~6%. Fed cuts pushed to 2027. Gold bid sustained. Headwind for high-multiple names but tech absorbing it.
- **Iran deal:** NOT confirmed. Oil $100-126/bbl Brent. XLE stop $56.15 (2.3% cushion — monitor).
- **NVDA earnings May 21 (7 days):** No new NVDA entries after May 19. Pre-earnings review mandatory Daily Review May 19. H200 approval = very positive earnings setup.
- **Bitcoin:** $79,549. Below $82K threshold. Crypto down 1.5% despite risk-on. Skip continues.

watchlist.notes: Micron makes DRAM/HBM memory used in AI servers. H200 approval = more AI server demand = more HBM demand. NVDA H200 uses HBM3e from SK Hynix primarily but Micron is #2 HBM supplier. Worth researching for limit order entry.
